name: Student Affairs at Vanderbilt
summary: Student Affairs serves a central role in student learning and development at Vanderbilt, advancing the University's mission of teaching, research, and service.

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers
summary: Since 1946, the Vanderbilt Student Chapter of the American Institute of Chemical Engineers has provided a platform of professional development opportunities for Vanderbilt University's undergraduate chemical engineering students.

name: American Society of Mechanical Engineers
summary: Vanderbilt ASME strives to provide meaningful activities, resources, and connections for undergraduate Mechanical engineers.

name: Vanderbilt Association of Student Anthropologists
summary: Vanderbilt Association of Student Anthropologists encourages anthropological conversation between students and faculty in a casual community, and provides opportunities to gain real-world anthropological experience.

name: Chemical Biology Association of Students (CBAS)
summary: We are an organization that strives to promote professional and social relationships between students, faculty, and post-doctoral fellows in the chemical biology discipline.

name: Chemical Engineering Graduate Student Association
summary: The purpose of ChEGSA is to enhance the experience of graduate students within the department by engendering camaraderie among the graduate students and increasing student involvement in the department through student/faculty interactions.

name: Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers
summary: SASE is dedicated to the advancement of Asian heritage scientists and engineers in education and employment so that they can achieve their full career potential.

name: Society of Women Engineers
summary: Empower women to achieve their full potential in careers as engineers and leaders; expand the image of the engineering and technology professions as a positive force in improving the quality of life, and demonstrate the value of diversity and inclusion.

name: Theta Tau
summary: Theta Tau is a co-ed professional engineering fraternity that emphasizes brotherhood, service, and professional development to cultivate well-rounded engineers dedicated to making a positive impact on society.

name: Vanderbilt Math Club
summary: Vanderbilt Math Club provides a place for those interested in mathematics to get to know each other on a more informal level outside of the classroom. Students can also find mentoring and learn about undergraduate opportunities.

name: Undergraduate Honor Council
summary: The Undergraduate Honor Council is run by students and supported by faculty advisers. The Executive Board and the general body, consisting of around 90 members, is entirely composed of students.

name: Vanderbilt Bar Association
summary: The VBA is composed of all students of Vanderbilt Law School. The organization's objectives are to foster a spirit of community and cooperation among students, faculty and staff, and to advance the aims and purposes of the student body at the law school.

name: Vanderbilt Student Government
summary: Vanderbilt Student Government’s mission is to enrich and improve the undergraduate experience through proactive advocacy on behalf of students, collaborative partnerships with administrators and faculty, and relevant, innovative services.

name: Dores for Israel (DFI)
summary: Dores for Israel is Vanderbilt's Israel advocacy group on campus. DFI promotes a positive impact on the Israeli climate on campus through organizing educational, political and social programs.

name: The National Association for the Advancement of Colored People
summary: The mission of the National Association for the Advancement of Colored People is to ensure the political, educational, social, and economic equality of rights of all persons and to eliminate race-based discrimination.

name: Vanderbilt Business Review
summary: The Vanderbilt Business Review is publication whose members write on a variety of topics that interest them. As the only undergraduate business publication, we service the Vanderbilt community by providing business coverage with a student focus.

name: Owen Global Business Association
summary: The Global Business Association (GBA) strives to link Owen to the world of global business where we live and work. Our vision is to provide tangible value to your Owen experience by increasing awareness about globally focused business environments.

name: Human and Organizational Performance Association
summary: Our mission at HOPA is to foster and support the professional development of prospective, current and alumni members of the HOP concentration at the Owen Graduate School of Management.

name: Operations Management Club, Owen Graduate School of Management
summary: The Operations Management Club at Owen is a club that enhances in-class learning and professional development by organizing lunch and learn panels with industry leaders, recruiting treks and tours to companies across multiple industries.

name: Owen Armed Forces Club
summary: The Owen Armed Forces Club represents all Owen Graduate School of Management students who have an interest in leadership and/or the military and provides a networking platform for students, alumni, and prospective candidates.

name: Owen Finance Club
summary: The OFC provides a forum for students to actively pursue finance-related interests including education and training, career development and personal network development in an educational-friendly environment.

name: Owen Partners Association
summary: The Owen Partners Association (OPA) is the official club that helps connect and integrate spouses, partners, and families to the Owen and Vanderbilt communities.

name: Owen Women's Business Association
summary: The Owen Women's Business Association (WBA) strives to create an inclusive and empowering environment for all women. We support the women of Owen through professional networking events and provide opportunities for personal and professional development.

name: Black Student Association
summary: The Black Student Association is dedicated to the social and academic uplift of Black students with a focus on educating campus about issues of diversity and inclusion, mainly those affecting Black and African American students.

name: La Alianza at Vanderbilt Law School
summary: La Alianza is a platform to offer support and a voice for Latinx/Hispanic students at Vanderbilt Law School. La Alianza offers an opportunity to support law students interested in exploring legal issues facing Latinx people in the U.S. and Latin America.

name: Vanderbilt Lambda Association
summary: The Vanderbilt Lambda Association is Vanderbilt’s undergraduate Gender and Sexuality Alliance. Lambda serves and supports LGBTQ+ students, in addition to providing a voice on LGBTQ+ matters on campus and within the greater Nashville community.

name: Owen Latin Business Association
summary: The Owen Latin American Business Association (LABA) is a student-run organization that brings together a community who is passionate about Latin America.

name: Middle Eastern and North African Student Association (Formerly MESA)
summary: The Middle Eastern and North African Students Association (MENA) is an undergraduate, student-run organization that provides a common ground for Middle Eastern and North African students to come together...

name: National Black Law Student Association
summary: The National Black Law Student Association (NBLSA) exists to articulate and promote the educational, professional, political, and social needs and goals of Black Pre-Law students; as well as, foster a community amongst Black pre-law individuals.

name: National Society of Black Engineers
summary: The mission of NSBE is to increase the number of culturally responsible black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community.

name: Organization of Black Graduate and Professional Students
summary: Graduate and professional student organization.

name: Peabody Coalition of Black Graduates
summary: The Peabody Coalition of Black Graduates is an organization geared toward Black students that seeks to provide social support to its members. PCBG also plays an integral in the recruitment and retention of Black students on Peabody's campus.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
summary: To implement programs for the advancement of Hispanic engineers in the University and in society.

name: South Asian Law Students Association
summary: The South Asian Law Students Association provides networking opportunities, scholarly programming, and social events for South Asian law students. To find out more or to join SALSA, contact any officer or the Office of Student Affairs.

name: Association of Latin American Students
summary: Association of Latin American Students is an organization at Vanderbilt University responsible for celebrating and promoting Latinx culture for all students. It is an actively involved organization on campus and works to make a lasting difference.

name: Engineering Council
summary: The Engineering Council serves as the governing body of the School of Engineering.

name: Anthropology Graduate Student Association (VAGSA)
summary: Organization for Graduate Students in the Department of Anthropology

name: English Graduate Students Association
summary: A professionalization and service organization for the graduate students in the English department.

name: Mechanical Engineering Graduate Student Association
summary: The mission of the Mechanical Engineering Graduate Student Association is to promote social and professional interaction among students within the mechanical engineering department while also serving to facilitate feedback between students and faculty.

name: Global Health Alliance
summary: The Global Health Alliance (formerly VIGH Student Advisory Council) brings together undergraduate and graduate students from all departments interested in global health, with the goal of fostering cross-disciplinary student networking and collaboration.

name: Phi Delta Phi
summary:
Phi Delta Phi is an international legal honors fraternity that is among the oldest legal organizations in North America.

Mission: Phi Delta Phi  promotes the highest standard of ethics and professionalism in law schools and the legal profession.

name: Psi Chi International Psychology Honors Society
summary: Psi Chi is the International Honor Society for Psychology majors. Membership is open to second-semester sophomores and up studying Psychology in both Peabody and the College of Arts and Sciences.

name: The Owen Cork & Barrel Society
summary: Through tastings, seminars and “spirited” events, The Owen Cork & Barrel Society offers business school students the opportunity to learn about and appreciate libations with classmates firsthand.

name: Criminal Law Association at Vanderbilt Law School
summary: The Criminal Law Association (CLA) seeks to promote both the study and the practice of criminal law. CLA sponsors lectures, forums, and debates on issues of current concern. Speakers include faculty members at the Law School, local and national attorneys

name: Law and Business Society of Vanderbilt Law School
summary: Mission Statement:  Vanderbilt Law and Business Society is committed to exposing students to scholarship and professionals at the intersection of business and the law, aiming to supplement the litigation-focused first-year law school curriculum.

name: Law Students for Social Justice
summary: Supports the mission of the Social Justice Program at Vanderbilt Law School through student-driven programming and events related to exploring the role of law in creating, perpetuating, and eradicating hierarchies of power and privilege in our society.

name: Vanderbilt/Fisk Universities Materials Research Society
summary: The Vanderbilt/Fisk Universities Chapter of the Materials Research Society (part of the national Materials Research Society) is for students interested in materials science or researching materials.

name: outLaw
summary: The purpose of outLaw is to utilize the collective resources of its members, including LGBTQQIAA law students, and the community in order to foster and encourage their academic success and professional competence.

name: Intellectual Property Association
summary: The Vanderbilt Intellectual Property Association supports the intellectual property community at the Vanderbilt University Law School. It organizes educational events, networking opportunities and social gatherings for students interested in IP.

name: Vanderbilt Mock Trial Organization
summary: Vanderbilt Mock Trial gives students an opportunity to learn about the legal process and compete in mock trial tournaments around the country. Students fill the roles of attorneys and witnesses as the team competes against other schools.

name: Moot Court Board at Vanderbilt Law School
summary: The Moot Court Board promotes the practice of effective appellate advocacy by leading all appellate advocacy activities at Vanderbilt Law School, including the Intramural Moot Court competition, traveling competitions, and the 1L oral argument program.

name: Women Law Students Association
summary: WLSA was organized to promote gender equality in the law, in legal education, and in the legal profession. The organization sponsors speakers, panels, and discussion groups, and encourages all members to get involved in community outreach.

name: The Slant
summary: The Slant is Vanderbilt's premier humor and satire publication since 1886.

name: Vanderbilt University Theatre
summary: Vanderbilt University Theatre (VUTheatre) is a student organization affiliated with the Vanderbilt University Department of Theatre. Membership is open to any undergraduate or graduate student at Vanderbilt University!

name: VPB Speakers Committee
summary: The Vanderbilt University Speakers Committee has the responsibility of enhancing the quality of student life and the Vanderbilt educational experience by attracting and presenting distinguished speakers to the University.

name: VPB's Late Night Programming Committee
summary: VPB's Late Night Programming Committee (formerly VenUe) offers late-night programming for Vanderbilt students in unique and enjoyable ways. Our goal is to provide entertainment otherwise unavailable to students as a part of the Vanderbilt nightlife.

name: Alternative Spring Break
summary: ASB is Vanderbilt's largest student-run organization and the premier alternative break program in the nation. For more info, visit our website: my.vanderbilt.edu/vandyasb/!

name: InterAxon
summary: InterAxon is a STEM service-based organization, centering around neuroscience and cognitive sciences. Our goal is to introduce local Nashville middle school students to the significance of the nervous system in everyday life.

name: Begin with Books
summary: Begin with Books seeks to instill a lifelong love of reading and literature in young people through becoming virtual literacy tutors for students in Metro Nashville Public Schools.

name: Vanderbilt Prison Project
summary: A service-based organization that aims to educate, equip, and inspire the Vanderbilt student body to work with the Nashville community for progress in the criminal justice system, and to serve, advocate for, and uplift those impacted by it.

name: Vanderbilt Tour Guides
summary: Tour Guides is a student-run organization that collaborates with the Office of Undergraduate Admissions to provide an honest student perspective to prospective students to interested in Vanderbilt University.

name: Vanderbilt Secular Student Alliance
summary: The Secular Student Alliance empowers secular students to proudly express their identity, build welcoming communities, promote secular values, and set a course for lifelong activism.

name: Vanderbilt Programming Board
summary: The Vanderbilt Programming Board (VPB) is comprised of the 6 largest programming organizations on campus: Homecoming Committee, Speakers Committee, CityVU, The Music Group, Late Nite Programming, and Vandy Fanatics.

name: Career Center
summary:
GAIN CLARITY ABOUT YOUR FUTURE

Let the Career Center help

name: The Ingram Commons
summary: The Martha Rivers Ingram Commons begins four transformative years of a Vanderbilt education. First-year students live and learn together in the 10 houses of The Ingram Commons, each guided by a faculty head of house who lives among the students.

name: Student Centers
summary: Student Centers at Vanderbilt is a department within the Office of the Dean of Students in Rand 307. Our professional and student staff manage reservations, operations, and events in Student Centers and auxiliary building

name: Center for Spiritual and Religious Life
summary: The Center for Spiritual and Religious Life serves the students, faculty and staff of Vanderbilt University.

name: Arts and Student Activities
summary: The Office of Arts and Student Activities (ASA) includes the major arts and campus programming initiatives on Vanderbilt’s campus.

name: Margaret Cuninggim Women's Center
summary: Celebrating women, empowering all.

name: Crawford House
summary: Located on the Martha Rivers Ingram Commons, Crawford House provides modern features in a classic building housing 150 first-year students in traditional double rooms. Students live on floors 2–6 of this six story building.

name: Hank Ingram House
summary: Hank Ingram House is located next to the Dean's Residence on The Martha Rivers Ingram Commons. "Hank House" provides modern features in a classic building housing approximately 290 first-year students in traditional double rooms.

name: Gillette House
summary: Located on The Martha Rivers Ingram Commons, Gillette House is residence for 220 first-year men and women living separately on floors 2–6.

name: Murray House
summary: Murray House is located on The Martha Rivers Ingram Commons. Murray House provides modern features in a classic building housing 150 first-year students in traditional double rooms.

name: Stambaugh House
summary: Stambaugh House is located on the Martha Rivers Ingram Commons. Stambaugh provides modern features in a classic building housing 162 first-year students in traditional double rooms.

name: Sutherland House
summary: Sutherland House is located on the Martha Rivers Ingram Commons. Sutherland provides modern features in a classic building housing 162 first-year students in traditional double rooms with men and women living on separate floors.

name: West House
summary: Located on the Martha Rivers Ingram Commons, West House provides modern features in a classic structure. West houses approximately 112 first-year men and women on separate floors, mostly in traditional double rooms.

name: McGill Project
summary: The McGill Project is a Living & Learning community dedicated to free thought, creative expression, and inclusive community.

name: McTyeire International House
summary: The McTyeire International House program promotes the use of foreign languages and the awareness of different cultures. McTyeire is located on the main campus and features 97 single rooms and its own cafeteria.

name: Project Dialogue
summary: Project Dialogue is a University-wide program that seeks to involve the entire Vanderbilt community throughout the academic year in public discourse and reflection connecting classroom learning with larger societal issues.

name: Holocaust Lecture Series
summary: Dedicated to reflection on ourselves and our society in the wake of the Holocaust, for over 40 years HMLS has brought notable scholars, Survivors, Rescuers and Liberators, and artistic examinations of the Holocaust to Vanderbilt’s campus.

name: Housing and Residential Experience (HRE)
summary: OHARE has responsibility for all 34 of the residence halls on campus, staffing and programming within the residence halls, living learning communities, facilities management, and the Office of Conferences.

name: Vanderbilt Production Services
summary: Provides Audio Visual and event technical production services, equipment and consulting for the Vanderbilt Community

name: Owen Golf Club
summary: Owen Graduate School of Management Golf Club

name: Biological Sciences Graduate Student Association
summary: The purpose of the Biological Sciences Graduate Student Association is to increase comradery between all students in Biological Sciences by holding social events throughout the school year and recruiting graduate students to the department.

name: National Student Speech, Language, & Hearing Association
summary: Vanderbilt NSSLHA is local chapter of national NSSLHA. It provides a forum for graduate students in human communication sciences to engage in professional development, community service, social events, DEI initiatives, and fundraising efforts.

name: Global Health Organization - Med School
summary: The School of Medicine's network for students interested in opportunities and careers in global health.

name: Vanderbilt Speaking Club (Formerly Vanderbilt Toastmasters)
summary: Vanderbilt Speaking Club - become a more persuasive speaker. All are welcome to and no prior experience is necessary!

name: Universities Allied for Essential Medicines - Vanderbilt
summary: UAEM believes that we, as students of a research institution, sit in a key position to ensure better access to pharmaceutical drugs for all by influencing research innovation, drug development, & transparency in negotiations both at Vanderbilt and in TN.

name: Peabody Professional & Graduate Student Association
summary: The PPGSA is comprised of diverse student representatives from all Peabody departments and programs. The goals of the PPGSA are to promote student voice at Peabody; to advise and assist with student support programming; and, to advocate for student needs

name: VPB Homecoming Committee
summary: The Homecoming Committee works to provide interesting activities to incite enthusiasm for Vanderbilt athletics and alumni relations. These events include spirit events, service activities, and the Outstanding Senior Award.

name: International Lens Film Series
summary: A Film Series Curated by the Vanderbilt Cinema & Media Arts Program

name: Energy and Environmental Law Society
summary: Vanderbilt University Law School's Energy and Environmental Law Society is a student organization for students who are concerned about energy and environmental issues, and/or interested in careers in energy or environmental law.

name: Black Law Students Association
summary: The purpose of BLSA is to utilize the collective resources of the members to articulate, promote, and develop the professional, academic, and social needs and goals of Black law students and foster professional competence.

name: Middle Eastern & North African Law Students Association
summary: MENALSA aims to situate the Vanderbilt Legal Community in a position that fosters informed and meaningful dialogue about the Middle East & North Africa.  MENALSA members will enjoy educational and cultural events relating to the MENA region.

name: IEEE Vanderbilt
summary: IEEE Vanderbilt provides an organization around which Vanderbilt students can share in their common interests for electrical engineering, computer engineering, and computer science in relation with the national IEEE organization.

name: Owen Student Government Association
summary: The Owen Student Government Association (OSGA) is the student government for Masters of Business Administration, Masters of Accounting, and Masters of Finance students at the Owen Graduate School of Management (OGSM) at Vanderbilt University.

name: Vanderbilt University American Nuclear Society Student Section
summary: Our mission is to advance the understanding and application of nuclear science and technology by providing students with educational, professional, and leadership opportunities.

name: Greater China Business Club
summary: The mission of the Greater China Business Club (GCBC) is to promote cross-cultural communications and explore shared business opportunities between Owen and Greater China.

name: Philippine Intercultural Student Organization
summary: PISO serves Vanderbilt University and the greater Nashville community by engaging in a multifaceted approach in empowering students and community members — both of Filipino descent or with earnest interest in Filipino culture.

name: Students Transitioning Relaxed and Natural Developing Sisterhood
summary: STRANDS is an organization dedicated to uplifting and empowering of Black women on Vanderbilt's campus.

name: Vanderbilt College Democrats
summary: College Democrats is a student group dedicated to establishing a liberal voice on campus and increasing student involvement in politics. We seek to promote the Democratic party platform and assist local and national politicians aligned with our ideology.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi, Omega Nu Chapter
summary: AKPsi is Vanderbilt's oldest and largest professional co-ed business fraternity. Comprised of a diverse group of students and professionals, we are dedicated to fostering a supportive, professional environment for young business leaders on campus.

name: Owen Venture and Entrepreneurship Club
summary: The Owen VEC promotes and supports entrepreneurial and venture capital activities within the Owen Graduate School of Management, at Vanderbilt University, and in the Nashville Community as a whole.

name: Student Executive Council (McGill)
summary: The McGill Project is a Living & Learning community dedicated to free thought, creative expression, and inclusive community.

name: Vanderbilt Undergraduate Research Journal
summary: The Vanderbilt Undergraduate Research Journal welcomes submissions from all four undergraduate schools. VURJ conforms to the highest standards of ethics and integrity in the review process to bring forth quality, well-developed thought to the forefront.

name: Dores Design
summary: Dores Design is a FREE design service for all students and student organizations at Vanderbilt University. We help your marketing pop by creating logos, posters, flyers, stickers, t-shirt designs, and more! Fill out a request form to work with us today.

name: Resident Advisers
summary:
A Resource Page for Resident Advisors
Click here to file an incident report

name: Blair School of Music
summary: The Blair School of Music serves as the focal point within Vanderbilt University for the study of music as a human endeavor and as a performing art.

name: Design For America - Vanderbilt
summary: Design For America is a network of student-led organizations seeking to create social change through interdisciplinary design.

name: Vanderbilt Brewing and Fermentation Science Club
summary: Vanderbilt Brewing and Fermentation Science Club is student run organization that promotes appreciation for the science, art, culture, and history of beer and other fermented products through meetings, events, tastings, and brewing.

name: Engineers Without Borders
summary: EWB-Vanderbilt supports community-driven development programs worldwide by collaborating with local partners to design and implement sustainable engineering projects, while creating transformative experiences and responsible leaders.

name: Office of Stewardship
summary: The Stewardship Office celebrates the generosity of donors to Vanderbilt through recognition, appreciation, and communication to inspire trust and nurture relationships that encourage future philanthropy.

name: Parents and Family Programs
summary: The Parents and Family Programs office serves as a link between the University and the parents of undergraduate students.

name: The Vanderbilt Hustler/vanderbilthustler.com
summary: Vanderbilt's official student newspaper and online media outlet. The Vanderbilt Hustler publishes daily during the school year on vanderbilthustler.com.

name: The Vanderbilt Review Literary Journal
summary: Vanderbilt's official undergraduate-run literary and arts journal, published in print and online, and featuring poetry, fiction, creative nonfiction, and visual art.

name: The Communication of Science & Technology
summary: The Communication of Science Technology is an undergraduate interdisciplinary major.

name: Tau Beta Pi
summary:
Integrity and Excellence in Engineering 
Tau Beta Pi is universally recognized as the premier engineering honor society.  It is the oldest, largest, and most prestigious, and it brings together all engineering disciplines.

name: Susan Gray-Murray House Fellowship Program
summary: The Susan Gray-Murray House Fellowship Program is a volunteer program based out of Murray House on Commons. Past and current Murray House residents volunteer to be paired up with a preschool buddy from the Susan Gray School to visit each week.

name: VIEW
summary: The VIEW program through the Office of Active Citizenship and Service provides students with 8-week public service internships with leading agencies and congressional offices in our nation’s capital.

name: Vanderbilt University Advanced Degree Consulting Club
summary: .The Vanderbilt University Advanced Degree Consulting Club (VUADCC) is led by PhD, MD and JD candidates at Vanderbilt University. We serve the Vanderbilt APD candidates and postdoctoral fellows who wish to explore consulting as a career.

name: Vanderbilt Kennedy Center
summary: The Vanderbilt Kennedy Center (VKC) mission is to facilitate discoveries and best practices that make positive differences in the lives of persons with developmental disabilities and their families

name: Study Abroad
summary: Learn more and apply for more than 100 study abroad programs and scholarships.

name: Chemical and Physical Biology Graduate Student Association
summary: The purpose of the CPB GSA is to promote professional and social interaction among graduate students within the Chemical and Physical Biology program while also serving to facilitate feedback between students and faculty members.

name: Student Organization Funding  Committee (Formerly SSF)
summary: The Student Organization Fund Committee allocates funding to student organizations on an annual basis.

name: Vanderbilt Law School
summary: Among the nation's leading law schools, Vanderbilt stands out for its pathbreaking approaches to preparing lawyers for the 21st century.

name: Vanderbilt Bookstore
summary: Official Bookstore for Vanderbilt University operated by Follett Higher Education.

name: Civil & Environmental Engineering Council
summary: The purpose of the CEE Council is to promote social and professional interaction among the CEE graduate students and to facilitate communication between the department students and faculty members.

name: Peabody Office of Student Life
summary: Our office promotes engagement and holistic well-being for all Peabody students through community building and co-curricular programming. We partner with programs and offices across the university to ensure student awareness of and access to resources.

name: Center for Latin American, Caribbean, and Latinx Studies
summary: The Center for Latin American, Caribbean, and Latinx Studies (CLACX) fosters research and public engagement that center on populations that cross and re-cross borders constructed by geography, linguistics, class, race, and gender.

name: Vanderbilt Satellite Club
summary: The primary objective of VUSat is to supplement and encourage student passion for space through a combination of technical projects, experiential learning, speaker events, and the discussion of space news, history, and various other relevant topics.

name: Medicine, Education, and Development for Low Income Families Everywhere
summary: MEDLIFE is an organization that partners with low-income communities across the globe, improving their access to medicine, education, & community development initiatives. We achieve our mission through Mobile Clinics, fundraising, & student involvement.

name: Global China Connection
summary: Global China Connection is a global network for university students and young professionals of all nationalities looking to engage China’s emergence in the world.

name: Vanderbilt Innovation and Entrepreneurship Society
summary: The Vanderbilt Innovation and Entrepreneurship Society (VINES) serves passionate innovators in the execution of ideas by providing resources, education, and an entrepreneurial community.

name: Department of Sociology
summary: Sociology is a vital and integral part of national and international profession. The thematic diversity of Vanderbilt sociology stems from the faculty's wide-ranging interests and methodological competencies and a strong commitment to academic freedom.

name: Owen Graduate School of Management
summary: Owen Graduate School of Management

name: Department of Mathematics
summary: College of Arts & Science Department of Mathematics

name: EmbrACE
summary: The purpose of EmbrACE (Aspire, Care, Empower) is to give female-identifying undergraduate students at Vanderbilt University the opportunity to regularly mentor elementary and middle school girls in the Nashville area on age- and gender-related issues.

name: Department of Economics
summary: The Department of Economics is in Vanderbilt’s College of Arts and Science. It consists of more than 30 full-time faculty whose research covers most major areas of modern economics.

name: Vanderbilt Student Academy of Audiology
summary: The Student Academy of Audiology is the national student organization of the American Academy of Audiology that serves as a collective voice for students and advances the rights, interests, and welfare of students pursuing careers in audiology.

name: Alumni Lawn- Cole & Tolman
summary: Cole and Tolman Halls are both located on central campus, and offer easy access to dining and classroom facilities.  Cole houses 116 upperclass women mostly in single rooms, and Tolman houses 102 upperclass men in single and double rooms.

name: Political Science Graduate Student Association
summary: The Political Science GSA works to promote excellence in scholarship and teaching amongst the graduate student body of the Department of Political Science. We create community within our department, and work to identify and solve common challenges.

name: STEM Tutoring
summary:
Free STEM Tutoring - Virtual/In-person. Drop-in/Appointment. Group/Individual. Offered during fall and spring. Tutors are Vanderbilt students. 
Penji Tutoring app or www.penjiapp.com. Log in with VU email. 
*This page is for info only.*

name: Chancellor's Scholars
summary: The Chancellor's Scholarships are offered to a select group of students who show a strong commitment to diversity, leadership, citizenship and scholarship. Scholars receive full tuition in addition to funding up to $6,000 for one summer experience.

name: Student Center for Belonging and Communities
summary: Student Center for Belonging and Communities (SCBC)

name: Institute for Software Integrated Systems
summary: Institute for Software Integrated Systems Office

name: Robert Penn Warren Center for the Humanities
summary: Our center promotes interdisciplinary research and study in the humanities and social sciences.

name: Labor and Employment Law Society at Vanderbilt Law School
summary: The Labor and Employment Law Society (LELS) seeks to educate and inform its members and the Vanderbilt Law community about L&E law issues and possible career paths at the local, state, federal and international levels.

name: VU SPIE Student Chapter
summary: SPIE, the international society for optics and photonics, was founded in 1955 to advance light-based technologies through interdisciplinary information exchange, continuing education, publications, patent precedent, and career and professional growth.

name: Vanderbilt University Libraries
summary: The Jean and Alexander Heard Libraries will be a leader, partner and ally in propelling knowledge to address the challenges and opportunities of a rapidly changing world.

name: Gender and Sexuality Studies
summary: Gender and Sexuality Studies is an interdisciplinary program that encourages the development of critical perspectives in both intellectual and activist contexts.

name: Undergraduate Political Science Association
summary: The Vanderbilt Undergraduate Political Science Association's mission is twofold. First, the group works to facilitate interactions between faculty and students. Second, the group works to enhance political science students' understanding of the field.

name: Owen Tech Club
summary: The purpose of the club is to educate and provide resources to students regarding careers in technology industry. The club would provide students with several platforms to network with Owen alumni and technology companies.

name: Vanderbilt University Women in Science and Engineering
summary: VU-WiSE is a community and support network of women in science, engineering and mathematics at Vanderbilt and their supporters.

name: National Society of Collegiate Scholars
summary: NSCS is an honors organization that recognizes and elevates high achievers. NSCS provides career and graduate school connections, leadership and service opportunities and gives out nearly a million dollars in scholarships.

name: MOSAIC
summary: Medley Of Students And Ideas Connecting (MOSAIC) Recruitment Committee exists as an extension of Undergraduate Admissions to connect prospective students to the Vanderbilt student perspective, as well as to provide community-building programming.

name: Susan Gray School
summary: Susan Gray School-Peabody College- Vanderbilt University

name: Department of Special Education
summary: Ranked as a top-tier special education program in the nation by U.S. News & World Report for more than a decade, the department has long been a leader both in research and practice.

name: POIESIS
summary: Ministry of the Arts

name: Department of Health Policy
summary: To inform health policy and to improve health and health care through research, education and service.

name: Peabody Q. Ed.
summary: Through intentional, community-building outreach, advocacy, social events, and education, Peabody Q.Ed. (Queering Education and Development) will make Peabody a safer, more inclusive environment for LGBTQI+ students and their allies.

name: Pharmacology Graduate Student Association
summary:
PhGSA strives to facilitate an understanding and appreciation of the
pharmacological sciences in both the campus and civic communities.

name: Society for the Advancement of Chicanos/Hispanics and Native Americans in Science
summary: SACNAS serves to increase the number of Chicanos/Hispanics and Native Americans in science by supporting them throughout their college, advanced education, and professional careers.

name: Brazilian Student Association (BRASA) at Vanderbilt University
summary: BRASA's goal is to promote opportunities for Brazilians studying outside of Brazil. Our mission is to connect Brazilian students studying abroad, empower the Brazilian community at Vanderbilt, and support professional development at Vanderbilt.

name: Vanderbilt Department of Art
summary: An important goal of the Art Department is to help students become lifelong interpreters of the rich visual environment in our culture as well as proponents of the creative approach in their selected professions, whatever they might turn out to be.

name: VandyHacks
summary: VandyHacks is an organization dedicated to fostering hacker culture and building a computer science community at Vanderbilt University. We accomplish this by hosting an annual hackathon as well as regular hack nights and study nights throughout the year.

name: Vanderbilt University Amateur Radio Club
summary: Student, faculty, and alumnus hub for amateur radio enthusiasts. Our goal is to both assist new students in becoming certified as ham radio operators and to provide a friendly community for VU-affiliated licensed and aspiring "hams."

name: Owen Out & Allied
summary: Our mission is to provide an open and supportive environment for the LGBTQ+ community at Owen. Out & Allied strives to strengthen allyship, promote an inclusive and diverse culture, and further the professional goals of our members.

name: AmbassaDores: Dore for a Day

summary: NMS supports, uplifts, and engages the new music community at Vanderbilt by programming and supporting student events and projects related to contemporary art music.

summary: The National Society of Black Women in Medicine is a multi-disciplined organization that works to increase the recruitment and retention of Black women pursuing broad careers in medicine.

name: Tech Law Society
summary: Tech Law Society seeks to raise student awareness of the legal issues associated with technology law (e.g, AI/ML, cryptocurrency, blockchain, IoT, FinTech, cybersecurity, data security, clean energy technology, biotechnology, healthcare technology, etc.)

name: Vanderbilt Pediatric Association
summary: The goals of VPA are to promote awareness and education of the Pediatric Field amongst its members and to keep the Vanderbilt Community informed on events and issues that affect the Pediatric Field through pediatric opportunities and mentorship.

name: Black Atlantic Speakers Series
summary: This series has hosted some of the most renowned scholars in the field to speak about their research, publications, and digital projects for Black History month.

name: Canta Canta
summary: Canta Canta is a student-led Vanderbilt nonprofit that creates and distributes handmade interactive art books to pediatric patients, especially from under-resourced, uninsured, and immigrant communities.

name: Baseball Analytics Association
summary: The Baseball Analytics Association will acquaint its members with the applications of statistical and computational tools in baseball analysis. Activities include educational meetings, general discussions, and project work/presentation.

name: IMA Vanderbilt
summary: For more than 100 years, The Institute of Management Accountants has been a champion of management accounting and finance professionals. Vanderbilt's chapter will give students the opportunity to become certified managerial accountants.

name: PDP Student Activites Board
summary: The VUSM Professional Degree Program students of the Vanderbilt community coming together for professional and social events and opportunities.

name: The Society of Vanderbilt Mathematics Graduate Students
summary:
This is an organization whose purpose it to foster community and promote professional
engagement among the Graduate Mathematics Students and anyone else at
Vanderbilt University who has passion for graduate level mathematics.

name: Vanderbilt Socio-Historical Society
summary: Guest lectures from topical academics, social events, opportunities to engage with graduate research, and guided visits to historical, architectural, and environmental spaces of historic and cultural importance.

name: Vanderbilt Partners for Parkinson’s Program
summary: The Partners for Parkinson’s Program fosters meaningful connections between students and individuals with Parkinson’s, aiming to reduce social isolation while providing volunteers with a deeper understanding of neurological movement disorders.

name: Nu Rho Psi
summary: The mission of Nu Rho Psi is to encourage professional interest and excellence in scholarship, particularly in neuroscience as well as promote career development in neuroscience and related fields.

name: American Lung Cancer Screening Initiative
summary: ALCSI raises awareness of lung cancer screening in Vanderbilt, Nashville, and Tennessee; connecting people to local screening programs; and embedding awareness through community outreach, health education, and policy/advocacy change.

name: Arts and Sciences: Beyond The Core
summary: The A&S College Core is the foundation for undergraduate study for College of Arts and Science. "Beyond the Core" is designed to help students explore their academic goals and intellectual passions.

name: School of Engineering
summary: The Vanderbilt School of Engineering is dedicated to creating a welcoming, connected community where students, faculty, and staff thrive together.

name: Institute for the Advancement of Higher Education
summary: The Institute for the Advancement of Higher Education (AdvancED) leads cutting-edge, research-backed innovations in pedagogy and student experience.

name: Housing and Relocation
summary: This program is a great resource for off-campus housing needs.

name: Center for Latin American, Caribbean and Latinx Studies
summary: Our Center offers a variety of programs and events for students throughout the academic year, and we would like to use Anchorlink as a resource to promote our events and remain connected with the campus community.

name: Vanderbilt University Institute of National Security
summary: In 2023, Vanderbilt began envisioning an Institute of National Security as part of the ambitious Discovery Vanderbilt, established to pursue vital solutions to the world's greatest challenges.

name: Legal Studies & Pre-Law Advising
summary: The Legal Studies team at Vanderbilt Law School manages the undergraduate Legal Studies minor, the Master of Legal Studies program, the international LL.M. program, and the university's Pre-Law Advising office.

name: Women, Law, and Policy Program
summary: The Women, Law, and Policy Program aims to explore the ways in which law and policy shape our experiences in leadership, in the workplace and across other spheres.

name: Fashion Law Society
summary: Fashion reflects cultural expression, and the Fashion Law Society’s seeks to provide a space where members can explore their interest in fashion and artistic creativity can enhance their law school experience and formative years in the legal field.

name: School for Science and Math at Vanderbilt
summary: School for Science and Math at Vanderbilt

name: Engine for Art, Democracy & Justice
summary: The Engine for Art, Democracy & Justice (or EADJ for short) is a research initiative started by Dr. María Magdalena Campos-Pons, an internationally renowned artist and the Cornelius Vanderbilt Endowed Chair & Professor of Fine Arts.
